name: The Red-Headed League
description: The Red-Headed League is an elaborate fiction invented by John Clay to keep Jabez Wilson occupied and away from his own pawnshop for several hours each day. Advertised in a newspaper as a well-endowed organisation paying four pounds a week to red-haired men willing to perform light clerical work, the 'League' operated from Saxe-Coburg Square under a man named Duncan Ross (another Clay accomplice). Its stated purpose — copying the Encyclopædia Britannica — was designed to sound absurd enough to attract little attention. When Clay's tunnel to the adjacent bank vault was complete, the League was dissolved by a notice on the door. Holmes notes that the scheme is distinguished by the audacity of its pretence and the elegance of its misdirection.
